name: ORION
description: The Hunter — one of the most recognizable winter constellations

name: SCORPIUS
description: The Scorpion — dominant summer constellation near the galactic center

name: CYGNUS
description: The Swan — part of the Summer Triangle, rich in nebulae

name: LYRA
description: The Lyre — home of Vega, one of the brightest stars

name: SAGITTARIUS
description: The Archer — points toward the galactic center

name: LEO
description: The Lion — prominent spring constellation with bright Regulus

name: VIRGO
description: The Maiden — second largest constellation, contains Spica

name: PEGASUS
description: The Winged Horse — autumn sky anchor

name: GEMINI
description: The Twins — Castor and Pollux mark the twin stars

name: TAURUS
description: The Bull — contains the Pleiades and Hyades clusters

name: ANDROMEDA
description: Home to the Andromeda Galaxy — closest galaxy to the Milky Way

name: AQUILA
description: The Eagle — anchors Altair of the Summer Triangle

name: URSA MAJOR
description: The Great Bear — home of the Big Dipper asterism

name: BOÖTES
description: The Herdsman — contains the bright star Arcturus

name: PERSEUS
description: The Hero — home to the famous Perseid meteor shower radiant
